user_outcome: A SwornAgent user resolves the same explicit direct provider or subscription driver before login, while logged in, and after logout, with no Sworn-managed route or bypass flag involved.
rationale: Account presence currently selects a proxy unless SWORN_DIRECT=1, and the registry separately advertises that implicit route. This slice owns C-04, deletes the proxy predicate and its host override, preserves customer-owned OpenRouter and other direct integrations, and makes the registry the only routing authority.
in_scope: Delete the Sworn account proxy endpoint, proxy-route predicate, bearer substitution, ViaProxy registry metadata, and capability output that advertises account-driven routing.
Remove every active read and current-help reference for SWORN_DIRECT and SWORN_PROXY_URL; introduce no recognized no-op or replacement routing flag.
Resolve in-process drivers only from explicit model prefixes, canonical provider configuration, and C-01; resolve subscription drivers only from their explicit CLI prefixes.
Preserve direct openrouter/, openai/, openai-completions/, deepseek/, groq/, mistral/, cloudflare/, github/, and anthropic/ behavior according to the canonical registry and provider configuration.
Preserve provider-side quota/credits error classification while removing account-proxy-specific error wrapping; S04 owns final user-message and product-surface cleanup.
Make capabilities enumeration and actual dispatch derive from the same registry entry without consulting account-session-v1.
Prove through real command construction that login/account records cannot alter the selected driver, endpoint, or credential source.
out_of_scope: Adding provider endpoints or dialects belongs to 2026-07-14-local-cloud-providers.
Removing account purchase, cached balance, TUI, MCP, and public credit surfaces belongs to S04-remove-managed-inference-credits.
Partner-managed inference requires a new ratified product/data boundary and is not preserved as dormant routing code.
Historical release specifications and proof bundles remain immutable evidence of prior behavior.

effort_complexity.rationale: Routing is duplicated across model construction, loop dispatch, driver enumeration, capability output, and many proxy-shaped tests, so deletion has a broad regression surface.

risks.description: Capabilities say direct while a lower dispatch layer still substitutes an account bearer or proxy endpoint.
risks.mitigation: One assembled test compares registry resolution, constructed request destination, and credential canary before/during/after account login.

risks.description: Removing managed proxy code accidentally removes customer-owned OpenRouter or another OpenAI-compatible provider.
risks.mitigation: Table-driven registry and request tests name every retained direct prefix, including openrouter/ with OPENROUTER_API_KEY and C-01.

risks.description: A compatibility test or helper keeps SWORN_PROXY_URL as a production credential redirect.
risks.mitigation: Use injected HTTP clients or explicit provider endpoint seams in tests and add an active-source guard that rejects both retired environment names outside historical artefacts.

acceptance_criteria.text: WHEN the built binary resolves and dispatches the same explicit in-process model before login, while C-02 is active, and after logout THE SYSTEM SHALL select the same registry driver, direct provider endpoint, and customer credential in all three states (C-01, C-02, C-04, N-02, N-04).

WHEN sworn capabilities enumerates in-process and subscription drivers THE SYSTEM SHALL report explicit prefixes and reachability without ViaProxy metadata, account-dependent alternatives, or SWORN_DIRECT guidance (C-04, N-02, N-03).

WHEN openrouter/<model> is configured with the customer's canonical environment key or C-01 value THE SYSTEM SHALL send directly to the configured OpenRouter endpoint and SHALL not require C-02 (C-01, C-04, N-04).
WHEN an explicit subscription-driver prefix is selected THE SYSTEM SHALL dispatch that CLI driver without reading provider-credentials-v1 or account-session-v1 (C-04, N-02, N-04).

IF SWORN_DIRECT or SWORN_PROXY_URL is present in the process environment THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL give it no routing, endpoint, credential, help, warning, capability, DNS, or HTTP behavior (C-04, N-02, N-03, N-07, N-09).

IF a direct provider returns a quota or credit-exhaustion response THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL retain the provider terminal classification without wrapping it in a Sworn account purchase or managed-proxy error (C-04, N-03).
